title: Composite Panel Manufacturing
entry_mechanism: PSAP Track 2 — JV with international composite aerostructures Tier 2 at KAEC composites platform
executive_summary:
Commercial aircraft structural panels — wing skin panels, fuselage panels, floor beams, and nacelle structures — are among the highest-value components in civil aircraft production. Node CA-2 targets the establishment of Saudi composite panel manufacturing for commercial aircraft, using the KAEC composites platform established by the military aerospace programme (MA-1) as its industrial foundation. The capability transfer from MA-1 (military composite sub-assemblies) to CA-2 (commercial aircraft panels) is the NCAS cascade logic in action: military manufacturing investment creating commercial manufacturing capability.

The addressable market is substantial: Airbus and Boeing collectively produce over 1,400 commercial aircraft per year, each consuming tonnes of composite material in structural panels. As a Tier 2 composite panel supplier, a Saudi manufacturer can target commercial aircraft programmes through the major international aerostructures primes — Stelia Aerospace, Premium AEROTEC, Spirit AeroSystems — who in turn supply Airbus and Boeing.

Saudi Arabia's advantages: KAEC's existing composites platform, SABIC's polymer materials supply chain, and a growing pool of composite technicians from the MA-1 programme. The constraint: AS9100 certification and Nadcap accreditation are prerequisites for commercial aerostructures supply — timelines of 24–36 months from programme start.

companies.name: Stelia Aerospace (Airbus Group)
companies.rationale: Stelia is a Tier 1 aerostructures supplier to Airbus and produces composite fuselage sections. A Stelia-Saudi JV at KAEC would provide immediate access to Airbus programme demand.
companies.contact: Stelia Aerospace VP Industrial Partnerships, International

companies.name: Sonaca
companies.rationale: Sonaca supplies composite panels to Airbus and Boeing and has active international manufacturing JV experience. Non-ITAR, Gulf-interested.
companies.contact: Sonaca VP Business Development, Middle East & Africa

companies.name: Spirit AeroSystems
companies.rationale: Spirit produces composite panels for Boeing 737 MAX and 787. ITAR considerations apply to some work packages but commercial composite panel technology is accessible.
companies.contact: Spirit AeroSystems VP International Industrial Partnerships

companies.name: Daher
companies.rationale: Daher produces composite aerostructures and aircraft and has Saudi Arabia industrial engagement interest.

companies.name: GKN Aerospace
companies.rationale: GKN is the world's largest independent aerostructures company. A GKN work package allocation to a Saudi composite manufacturer would be transformative.
companies.contact: GKN Aerospace VP Supply Chain, International

partners.name: SAMI Composites / KAEC Platform
partners.current: Phase I composite capability from MA-1; AS9100 certification in progress; CFRP layup and autoclave
partners.upgrade: Expand to commercial aircraft panel geometry; AS9100 renewed for commercial scope; Nadcap accreditation; Stelia or Sonaca JV; first A320 family panel delivery
partners.timeline: 30 months from MA-1 Phase I completion

partners.name: SABIC Composites
partners.current: CFRP prepreg research; polymer matrix supply
partners.upgrade: Commercial-grade prepreg supply to Saudi panel manufacturer; Airbus-qualified material supplier; Saudi material content in commercial aircraft

partners.name: KAEC Industrial Authority
partners.upgrade: Dedicated aerospace composites cluster at KAEC; shared NDT, autoclave, and clean room infrastructure; AS9100 cluster certification framework

partners.name: KFUPM Aerospace / Composites Engineering
partners.upgrade: Commercial aerostructures composites engineering programme; Stelia-KFUPM industrial placement; Nadcap specialist training pipeline
